subject: Computer Networking
title: SPF — Sender Verification
description: A short lesson to help you apply SPF — Sender Verification.

hook.text: Anyone can write any From address in an email. SPF is the first check that catches the liars.

buildup.text: SPF — Sender Policy Framework — is a DNS record listing IPs allowed to send for a domain.

discovery.text: The receiving server checks: did this email come from an IP listed in the sender's SPF record?

twist.text: SPF breaks when email is forwarded. The forwarding server's IP isn't in the original domain's SPF record.

climax.text: SPF alone isn't enough. It checks the envelope sender, not the From header the user sees.

punchline.text: SPF asks: is this server allowed to send for this domain?

quiz.question: Where is a domain's SPF record published?
options: In DNS as a TXT record
In the SMTP server's config file
